title: Mining and quarry workers and related operatives
description: Perform a variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers. Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems. (Based on 52 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Complete forms describing tasks completed.
Guide aircraft to designated areas using hand signals, batons, or other methods.
Inspect aircraft components to locate cracks, breaks, leaks, or other problems.
Clean aircraft interiors by picking up waste, wiping down windows, or vacuuming.
Change aircraft oil, coolant, or other fluids and maintain engines and tools.
Monitor pumps and flow lines for gas and fluid leaks and ensure equipment performance.
Prepare reports of services rendered, tools used, or time required for billing purposes.
Direct drilling crews and supervise other workers engaged in activities related to extraction.
Inspect derricks, clean and oil them to maintain proper working conditions.
Maintain and adjust machinery, perform safety inspections, and repair tools or equipment as needed.
Dismantle and repair oil field machinery and restore sites after extraction activities.
Drive truck-mounted units to well sites and transport materials necessary for drilling operations.
Guide cranes to move loads about decks during extraction operations.
Observe and monitor equipment operation during extraction processes to detect problems.
Clean up work areas and remove debris after extraction activities are complete.
Load materials into well holes or into equipment using hand tools.
Couple or uncouple trailers by changing trailer jack positions, connecting or disconnecting air or electrical lines, or manipulating fifth-wheel locks.
Collect delivery instructions from appropriate sources, verifying instructions and routes.
Check conditions of trailers after contents have been unloaded to ensure that there has been no damage.
Inspect loads to ensure that cargo is secure and check all load-related documentation for completeness and accuracy.
Drive trucks to weigh stations before and after loading and along routes in compliance with state regulations.
Operate equipment, such as truck cab computers, CB radios, and GPS, to exchange information with bases, supervisors, or other drivers.
Remove pieces of stone from larger masses using jackhammers, wedges, or other tools and insert wedges and feathers into holes to split stone sections from masses.
Reposition machines to make additional holes or cuts and operate mining machines to gather coal and convey it to floors or shuttle cars.
Conduct methane gas checks to ensure breathing quality of air and perform safety checks on equipment before operating.
Perform ventilation tasks, such as hanging ventilation curtains and tubes, and monitor air pressure, rotary speed, and pressure during drilling.
Light fuses, drop detonating devices into boreholes, or activate firing devices to set off blasts to demolish structures or displace earth, rock, or materials.
Monitor operation of cutting or slicing machines to detect malfunctions and remove defective materials from machines to ensure standards are met.
Assemble and position machines, explosives, and blasting caps in holes at specified depths, or load perforating guns with explosives.
Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites, maintaining inventory levels and ordering supplies as necessary.
Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as stone, glass, or rubber.
Examine, measure, and weigh materials or products to verify conformance to specifications, using measuring devices like rulers or scales.
Feed stock into cutting machines, or under cutting blades, by guiding or pushing it into position.
Clean and lubricate cutting machines, conveyors, and blades using appropriate tools and materials.
Operate or tend machines to mix or blend a variety of materials, such as food products or chemicals.
Monitor loading processes to ensure that materials are loaded according to specifications, verifying levels of excavated materials.
Drive loaded shuttle cars to discharge loads into mine cars or onto conveyors, and operate machinery to open and close canal locks.
Record operational or production data and maintain logs or records related to material movement.
Collect samples of materials or products for laboratory testing, ensuring compliance with specifications.
Inspect and adjust crane mechanisms or lifting accessories to prevent malfunctions and ensure safety.
Become familiar with digging plans, machine capabilities and limitations, and safe digging procedures.
Operate machinery to perform activities such as backfilling excavations and breaking rock or concrete.
Observe operation of equipment to ensure safety, efficient operation, and to detect malfunctions.
Examine and inspect machines to detect malfunctions and ensure conformance to established standards.
Clean, adjust, and maintain equipment using hand tools and ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Monitor and record processing data, materials used, and adjust controls to maintain specified conditions.
Prepare and transfer materials between work areas using moving equipment and hand tools.
Install and repair components of machinery or equipment, ensuring proper performance and compliance with specifications.
Perform routine maintenance on industrial equipment, including cleaning and lubricating components.
Diagnose faults or malfunctions to determine required repairs, using diagnostic equipment and testing devices.
Observe machine operations, gauges, or thermometers, and record gauge readings and processing times.
Collect samples and perform tests or laboratory analyses to assess quality of materials, such as biofuels.
Read work orders and specifications to determine equipment requiring repair or maintenance and schedule as needed.
Inspect components such as bearings and seals for damage or excessive wear to determine repair needs.
Reassemble equipment after completion of inspections, repairs, or testing, ensuring operability and compliance.
Disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts and make repairs, using hand tools, chain falls, jacks, or hoists.
Repair and maintain electrical and electronic controls for machinery, including checking and replacing defective parts with appropriate tools and equipment.
Perform scheduled maintenance, clean units and components, and lubricate or apply adhesives to machinery according to specified procedures.
Inspect or test machinery and equipment to identify defects and assess wear, reporting needs for repair or replacement directly.
Record details of repairs and maintenance performed, including parts used and operational conditions, for documentation purpose.
Supervise, coordinate, or schedule the activities of construction or extractive workers, ensuring compliance with safety standards and specifications.
Train and instruct new workers in machine operation, safety procedures, and maintenance tasks relevant to their roles.
Purchase or rent equipment necessary for construction or installation projects and requisition new materials as needed.
Collaborate with other workers to repair or move machines and maintain efficient operation of the workplace, assisting as necessary.
Analyze test results and reports to diagnose equipment problems, implementing solutions or adjustments to rectify issues.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as time and production records, inventory data, or maintenance statistics.
Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, or disciplinary measures.
Confer with personnel, such as management and representatives, to coordinate work activities, resolve grievances, or identify resource needs.
Investigate accidents or injuries, prepare reports of findings, and conduct worker training in safety or operational procedures.
Interpret specifications, blueprints, or job orders to guide workers and determine necessary installations or repairs.
Determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities based on priorities, equipment, and personnel skills.
Requisition materials, supplies, or personnel necessary for work activities and ensure adequate tool and inventory conditions.
Inspect work areas and equipment to ensure safety, compliance with regulations, and to prevent unsafe conditions.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to achieve production goals and meet operational demands.
Prepare and submit reports on work activities, operations, or accidents to relevant parties.
Drive vehicles or operate equipment to assist workers in completing job assignments.
Examine materials to ensure they meet safety standards and specifications, and to determine handling requirements.
Monitor field work to ensure performance quality and compliance with established standards and safety regulations.
Train workers in proper operational procedures, explain policies, and demonstrate tasks to ensure understanding.
Resolving worker issues collaboratively and improving motivational strategies for better personnel performance.
